active_risks.description: Chronology-oriented legacy artifact names can obscure whether claims, tests, evidence, and runtime capability metadata are target scoped and independently certifiable. Active tests, SSOT rows, Tier 3 report paths, retained-boundary metadata, current document-authority projections, and runtime status values are guarded; dirty-worktree release evidence and broader legacy generator/report surfaces remain release-blocking.
active_risks.title: Legacy provenance evidence ambiguity

all_partial_or_absent_features.description: Record a reproducible pre-token authorization decision artifact from normalized inputs and policy state.
all_partial_or_absent_features.title: Authorization decision trace artifact

all_partial_or_absent_features.description: Emit a stable authorization-decision hash that can be reproduced independently of final JWT bytes.

all_partial_or_absent_features.description: Fail closed on secret-bearing evidence, require governed redaction markers, and exclude compromised artifacts from active certification proof chains.

certification_blockers: Tier 4 independent peer validation is not complete for the retained boundary.
The peer-bundle completeness gate is not satisfied for the declared peer-profile set.
One or more supported peer profiles have incomplete or invalid preserved external evidence bundles.
Validated in-scope certification lane execution evidence is incomplete or missing.
At least one claim row is still missing a machine-derived certification proof binding.
Release evidence can now be built only from a clean checkout, and the current workspace is dirty.

open_issues.description: Active SSOT test rows, active test sources, Tier 3 evidence report paths, retained-boundary generated bundle metadata, copied retained-boundary release-bundle paths, package runtime status values, and current document-authority projections now use capability-scoped names. Certification remains blocked until dirty-worktree release evidence is replaced by clean-checkout evidence and broader legacy generator/report surfaces are normalized or archived.
open_issues.title: Active provenance naming blocks certification

open_issues.description: JWT bytes should remain nondeterministic, but pre-token authorization decisions need a durable deterministic artifact with normalized request hashing, policy hashing, derivation steps, and decision linkage that can be reproduced independently of token timestamps and signatures.
open_issues.title: Authorization decisions need deterministic provenance artifacts before token minting

open_issues.description: The repo still states that certifiability is not closed because validated runtime-matrix evidence, migration portability truth, and independent Tier 4 proof are incomplete. That status should be tracked as an explicit SSOT blocker rather than only as narrative in reports.
open_issues.title: Certification closure remains blocked by runtime matrix truth and independent evidence

open_issues.description: The published admin JSON-RPC table surface method Client.clear returns JSON-RPC error -32603 Internal error even after retrying with schema-shaped params. This is a runtime defect in the generated clear path rather than an expected invalid-params rejection.
open_issues.title: Client JSON-RPC clear returns internal error

open_issues.description: Static discovery profile snapshots can drift from live deployment-derived metadata. Published discovery artifacts must either be generated from the same runtime deployment resolver used by the app or be clearly marked as non-authoritative fixtures.
open_issues.title: Published discovery artifacts must be runtime-derived or explicitly fixture-only

open_issues.description: OpenID Federation and SPIFFE/SPIRE are adjacent trust-domain targets, but they are not current claimable conformance surfaces in this repository. The SSOT needs explicit tracked-target rows and wording controls so those standards are not implied prematurely.
open_issues.title: External trust-domain targets must be tracked explicitly and not claimed early

open_issues.description: The published admin REST table endpoint PUT /logoutstate/{item_id} returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error for a synthetic UUID probe where the rest of the table surface fails closed. Replace must return a contract-valid response instead of crashing.
open_issues.title: LogoutState REST replace returns 500

open_issues.description: The published admin JSON-RPC table surface method PushedAuthorizationRequest.create returns HTTP 204 with an empty body after retry, which violates the expected JSON-RPC response envelope contract.
open_issues.title: PushedAuthorizationRequest JSON-RPC create returns empty 204

open_issues.description: The published admin REST table endpoint PUT /pushedauthorizationrequest/{item_id} returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error for a synthetic UUID probe. Replace must fail closed with a contract-valid status instead of crashing.
open_issues.title: PushedAuthorizationRequest REST replace returns 500

open_issues.description: Protected-resource metadata is present, but verifier semantics remain distributed across endpoint code paths. Define resource contracts that compile accepted issuers, audiences, token classes, proof requirements, and introspection caller policy into both runtime verification and RFC 9728 metadata.
open_issues.title: Protected resource contracts must compile runtime verifiers and metadata

open_issues.description: The published admin REST table endpoint PUT /revokedtoken/{item_id} returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error for a synthetic UUID probe. Replace must fail closed with a contract-valid status instead of crashing.
open_issues.title: RevokedToken REST replace returns 500

open_issues.description: Public, admin, RPC, and diagnostics surfaces are still composed into one runtime and separated mainly by flags, paths, and authorization gates. The repository needs a governed hard-isolation tier plus explicit downgrade classification for mixed-mode deployments.
open_issues.title: Runtime planes require a hard isolation tier and mixed-mode downgrade classification

open_issues.description: The published admin JSON-RPC table surface method Tenant.clear returns JSON-RPC error -32603 Internal error even after retrying with schema-shaped params. This is a runtime defect in the generated clear path rather than an expected invalid-params rejection.
open_issues.title: Tenant JSON-RPC clear returns internal error

open_issues.description: The published admin REST table endpoint PUT /tenant/{item_id} returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error for a synthetic UUID probe. Replace must fail closed with a contract-valid status instead of crashing.
open_issues.title: Tenant REST replace returns 500

open_issues.description: Resource-server interoperability remains under-proven because current Tier 4 bundles are not yet qualifying as independent verifier evidence. The repo needs a governed independent peer surface for protected-resource verification and replay/sender-constraint behavior.
open_issues.title: Tier 4 resource-server interoperability requires independent peer evidence

open_issues.description: Token exchange emits immediate actor context, but the system lacks a durable delegation graph or event history capturing parentage, attenuation, proof binding, audience or resource narrowing, and issued-token lineage for later verification and audit.
open_issues.title: Token exchange delegation provenance must be stored as a durable graph

open_issues.description: The published admin REST table endpoint PUT /tokenrecord/{item_id} returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error for a synthetic UUID probe. Replace must fail closed with a contract-valid status instead of crashing.
open_issues.title: TokenRecord REST replace returns 500

open_issues.description: Tenant discovery and tenant JWKS publication exist, but issuance and verification still need an explicit trust-domain model for issuer identity, keyset scope, allowed algorithms, and cross-tenant verification rejection.
open_issues.title: Tenant trust domain model required for issuer and signing-key selection

open_issues.description: The published admin JSON-RPC table surface method User.clear returns JSON-RPC error -32603 Internal error even after retrying with schema-shaped params. This is a runtime defect in the generated clear path rather than an expected invalid-params rejection.
open_issues.title: User JSON-RPC clear returns internal error
